IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control

Data analysis
Biostatistics
Survival analysis
PK/PD modelling
Clinical research
Decision-Making
Efficacy assessment of new formulation of Ivermectin : pre-clinical phase.

Context

This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).

Objectives

  • Identify the optimal formulation and dose meeting WHO Preferred Product Characteristics (HR > 4 for at least one month)
  • Evaluate efficacy in semi-field conditions (cattle–wild mosquitoes)
  • Estimate the epidemiological impact on malaria prevalence
  • Assess resistance development in Anopheles and Aedes
  • Study potential behavioural effects on mosquito host-seeking

My role

  • Designed study protocols, including sample size estimation and power calculations
  • Developed and implemented the statistical analysis plan
  • Performed data management, cleaning, and quality control
  • Conducted statistical analyses (survival models, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD, dose-response; epidemiological model)
  • Supervised students and supported partners in data analysis and interpretation
  • Communicated results to diverse stakeholders (scientific and non-scientific audiences)

Methods and tools

  • Survival analysis, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD modelling, non-linear dose-response, epidemiological model SAP, DMP redaction, study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation
  • R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, coxme, mgcv, drc, PKNCA)
  • Data management and reporting (R, Excel, Word, Quarto)
  • Study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation

Key Results

  • Sustained mosquitocidal efficacy for at least two months with HR > 4, meeting WHO PPC
  • Repeated treatment campaigns significantly reduced malaria prevalence (up to ~78%)
  • Evidence of differential resistance between Aedes and Anopheles
  • No significant effect on host-seeking behaviour

Impact

Contributed to the development of a novel malaria control strategy aligned with WHO recommendations Supported the selection of a lead formulation, enabling progression to Phase I clinical trials Provided key insights for deployment strategies, including resistance management

Publications

Sagna et al. (2023) ; Pooda et al. (2026) ; Zela et al. (2026) ; Dongmo et al. (2025)

References

Dongmo, Franck J, Angelique Porciani, Nicolas Moiroux, Hermann S Pooda, Roch K Dabiré, Samuel Beneteau, Charles S Wondji, Karine Mouline, and Ramsès Djidjou-Demasse. 2025. “Modeling the Impact of Ivermectin-Based Optimal Strategies on Malaria Control: The Role of Formulation, Coverage, and Mosquitocidal Efficacy Timing.” https://hal.science/hal-05335342.
Pooda, Sié Hermann, Abdoul Malik Bandaogo, Prudenciène Ablawa Agboho, Angélique Porciani, Samuel Beneteau, Lamidi Zela, Ali Nourou Ramzy Kambou, et al. 2026. “Impact of Ivermectin-Blood Meals from Treated Cattle on Mortality and Reproductive Parameters of Aedes Aegypti (Diptera: Culicidae) for Vector Control.” Journal of Medical Entomology 63 (1): tjag005. https://doi.org/10.1093/jme/tjag005.
Sagna, André B., Lamidi Zéla, Cheick Oumar W. Ouedraogo, Sié H. Pooda, Angélique Porciani, Joanna Furnival-Adams, Paula Lado, et al. 2023. “Ivermectin as a Novel Malaria Control Tool: Getting Ahead of the Resistance Curse.” Acta Tropica 245 (September): 106973. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.actatropica.2023.106973.
Zela, Lamidi, Sié Hermann Pooda, Angélique Porciani, Samuel Beneteau, André Barembaye Sagna, Sophie Le Lamer-Déchamps, Nicolas Moiroux, et al. 2026. “Selection of the Optimal Long-Acting Injectable Formulation of Ivermectin for Use in Humans to Target Malaria Vectors in Western Africa: Evaluation of Pharmacokinetics and Mosquitocidal Efficacy in Cattle Under Laboratory Conditions.” bioRxiv. https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.07.23.665952.
